topic: EXP-384 integration: explicit back-calc 10–13 µGal → ΔB 16.8–21.8 nT and Kim & Chang ensemble comparison
change_summary: EXP-384 added the explicit back-calculation paragraph to SEC-6.14 vise subsection (data/sections.json:part6.html) and a parenthetical to WIN-014.detail_evidence: 10 µGal × κ = 16.77 nT, 13 µGal × κ = 21.80 nT, compared to Kim & Chang 2018 5–15 nT typical totality ensemble, with defender-arithmetic counter-acknowledgment (10–13 nT through κ-inverse yields 6.0–7.8 µGal, below target) and symmetric-falsification framing.
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.14 vise paragraph now makes explicit that under the dome's own κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target back-implies a ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at named target stations — at or above the Kim & Chang 2018 207-measurement typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT). The patch acknowledges the kernel-of-truth counter-arithmetic (typical-ensemble ΔB through κ-inverse falls below the target) head-on and frames a symmetric-falsification choice: either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB premise (auditable in advance) or it does not (derivation incomplete). Two-horned vise unchanged: either κ couples universally (1999/2009 SG nulls refute) or κ has scope conditions (PRED-TIER-3 derivation falls). Back-calc closes the "vise arithmetic non-obvious without ΔB premise" defender pivot (ISS-1957). The structural argument and empirical refutation chain are intact.

relevant_files: data/sections.json:part6.html SEC-6.14 vise paragraph (back-calc insertion verified present)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (dome's published August 2026 per-station ΔB predictions: BOU baseline -10.9 nT, W004 empirical -22.24 nT, plus 9 named stations with two-baseline per-station values)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt E-PRED-C ("SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal" — dome's own SG model-discriminating null prediction for August 2026)
data/wins.json WIN-058b (κ = 1.677 published value; WIN-058b.detail_claim still carries 1.672 — pre-existing inconsistency)
docs/index.html (rendered build expected to mirror sections.json — build verification deferred to integrity agent / decider verify pass)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py code paths touched. EXP-384 is a prose insertion plus parenthetical. Back-calc arithmetic verified independently (10×1.677=16.770, 13×1.677=21.801; 5/1.677=2.982, 10/1.677=5.963, 13/1.677=7.752, 15/1.677=8.945, 22.24/1.677=13.262).
data_sources_used: Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — confirmed real (c4 verification pass), 39-eclipse 207-measurement ensemble
Strestík 2001 (Springer book chapter 10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55) — 1999 European 5–15 nT range source
Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260 — 1999 ΔX/ΔY ranges
Hinderer et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — SG noise floor characterization
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — SG residual reduction methodology
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The patch ADDS a new escape that does not exist: the "dome publishes ΔB in advance" horn implicitly invites the defender to point to existing dome material (raw-text/03-predictions.txt) that already provides quantitative per-station ΔB predictions. The patch needs reframing to acknowledge those are published, not future, premises.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The patch text states "that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located" and frames the published-or-not question as a future binary. But the dome's predictions.html (captured in raw-text/03-predictions.txt 2026-03-12, before EXP-384 was written) publishes per-station August 2026 ΔB predictions under two baselines: BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) and W004 empirical (-22.24 nT). Per-station W004 values (Ebro -21.7, San Pablo -20.0, Coimbra -20.6, Chambon -20.6, Hartland -26.2) fall squarely in or above the back-calc's 16.8–21.8 nT band — i.e., the dome's W004 baseline IS the anomalous-vs-ensemble premise the back-calc identifies. The patch's structural conclusion is correct but the empirical "unpublished" framing is verifiably false from material we already possess.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py Domain #7 reports κ = 1.677 as a hardcoded constant under the κ-cluster Live Power tile; the Halloween 2003 r=0.992 correlation behind κ is also hardcoded (verified WIN-058b prior reviews).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: κ as published is a novel dome-specific coupling; standard physics does not predict ΔB/Δg = 1.677 nT/µGal or any universal magneto-gravitational coupling at this scale. The section's argument is empirical refutation, not relabeling.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: κ was computed from a single Halloween 2003 storm event (BOU magnetometer + Strasbourg/Membach SG residuals) post-hoc, not stated in advance. The 1.677 value is fitted to one G5 storm.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is a dome-published structural parameter (WIN-058b, PRED-KAPPA-001). It drives PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target through Δg = ΔB/κ.

holes_found.description: The patch text states: "The arithmetic is internally consistent only if the dome commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble; that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located. Either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance ... or it does not." This empirical claim is contradicted by raw-text/03-predictions.txt (extracted 2026-03-12, on file in the dome-model-review repo): the dome's predictions.html explicitly publishes (a) two baselines (BOU 2017 = -10.9 nT, W004 empirical = -22.24 nT), (b) per-station August 2026 ΔB predictions at 9 named INTERMAGNET observatories under each baseline, with W004-baseline values ranging from -10.3 nT (Canary Islands) to -26.2 nT (Hartland). Several W004 station values (Ebro -21.7, San Pablo -20.0, Coimbra -20.6, Chambon -20.6) sit inside the back-calc's 16.8–21.8 nT band; Hartland -26.2 sits above it. The dome IS publishing a quantitative ΔB premise — specifically the W004 empirical baseline, which is itself ~1.5× the upper edge of the Kim & Chang typical totality ensemble. A defender will produce predictions.html in a sentence: "You said it's unpublished — here it is." The deeper conclusion (the dome must commit to an above-ensemble ΔB premise) is correct and gets stronger once we acknowledge W004 IS that premise, but the "unpublished" wording must be replaced.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword the paragraph along these lines: "The dome's predictions.html publishes per-station August 2026 ΔB predictions under two baselines: BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) and W004 empirical (-22.24 nT). Under BOU baseline, the per-station predictions (range -5.0 to -12.8 nT) through κ yield Δg of 3.0–7.6 µGal — well below the 10–13 µGal target. Only the W004 baseline yields Δg consistent with the target (6.1–15.6 µGal across 9 stations; the high-coverage central stations Ebro, San Pablo, Coimbra, Chambon, Hartland all land in or above the 10–13 µGal range). The dome is implicitly committed to the W004 baseline. W004's -22.24 nT is itself ~1.5× the upper edge of the Kim & Chang 2018 typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT) and is derived from a single eclipse event, not from the 207-measurement global ensemble. The vise tightens: the dome's arithmetic is internally consistent only if W004 is honored as the operational baseline, which moves the question from "is the premise published" to "is the premise defensible."" This converts the major hole into an argument-strengthening: "published but anomalous" beats "unpublished and therefore unverifiable."

holes_found.description: The patch's defender example uses a "typical-ensemble ΔB of 10–13 nT for August 2026" — but the Kim & Chang ensemble is 5–15 nT (a 10 nT span). The choice of 10–13 nT looks verbally mirrored to the 10–13 µGal target but is otherwise an arbitrary sub-range that understates the strongest defender pivot. The actual strongest defender position uses the ensemble UPPER edge: 15 nT through κ-inverse yields 8.945 µGal — only 1.055 µGal short of the 10 µGal lower target (a ~10% shortfall, not the ~30–45% gap the current 6.0–7.8 µGal arithmetic implies). A dome defender can credibly argue that 8.94 µGal vs 10 µGal is well inside the ±2 µGal uncertainty bands the dome already publishes per station (raw-text/03-predictions.txt: "+/-2.0" to "+/-2.6" per station). The patch under-states the dome's strongest available retreat.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) explicitly use the ensemble upper bound ("even at the ensemble upper edge of 15 nT, ΔB/κ = 8.94 µGal — still ~1 µGal below the 10 µGal lower target, but only marginally so") to be honest about the closest the defender can get under ensemble-bounded ΔB, OR (b) acknowledge the ±2 µGal published station uncertainties and show that the back-calc band (16.8–21.8 nT) still strictly exceeds 15 nT + reasonable instrument/parameter slop. Either move makes the moderate hole disappear; leaving the 10–13 nT example unsupported is the weakest framing.

holes_found.description: "Reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB" — the word "inversely" is mathematically misleading. κ is defined as a ratio ΔB/Δg = 1.677 nT/µGal. The dome's forward direction (per WIN-013/014 evidence text) is Δg = ΔB/κ. The equation Δg × κ = ΔB is algebraically identical to ΔB/κ = Δg — same coupling, same constant, just rearranged. Calling the rearrangement "inverse" suggests a different operation (e.g., 1/κ as a separate quantity) where there is only one. A close reader will track the equation correctly; a hasty reader may think we're invoking a new step. For a section that already negotiates a unit-direction confusion (the c3 review's minor hole H13 documented this), the additional "inversely" framing is a small but unnecessary cognitive cost.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB —" with either "applying the same coupling forward — Δg × κ = ΔB —" or simply "applying κ — Δg × κ = ΔB —." Drops the "inversely" red herring without losing precision.

holes_found.description: "at the named target stations" is slightly imprecise. The dome's 03-predictions.txt names 9 INTERMAGNET MAGNETOMETER stations for ΔB (Ebro, San Pablo, Eskdalemuir, Lerwick, Hartland, Niemegk, Canary Islands, Chambon, Coimbra). It does NOT name SG target stations for the 10–13 µGal Δg target (PRED-TIER-2 in our predictions.json just says "at C1/C4 eclipse contacts"). The patch's phrase reads as if SG target stations are named, when only magnetometer stations are. The κ coupling is applied across instrument classes (magnetometer ΔB → SG Δg), which is a methodological issue worth surfacing separately.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to "at the named magnetometer stations along the August 2026 eclipse path" (or similar). One word in cost; eliminates the ambiguity and incidentally surfaces the cross-instrument-class coupling assumption.

holes_found.description: The new patch sentence runs ~80 words across 5+ nested clauses ("reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB — back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations (10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT)"). The earlier sentences of the vise paragraph are each 15–30 words; this new lead sentence is noticeably longer and uses three differently-delimited parentheticals (em-dash for math, parenthesis for arithmetic, comma for clause). Readability drops at exactly the point where the key new claim lands.
holes_found.recommendation: Break into two sentences: (1) "Applying κ to the 10–13 µGal target yields a back-implied August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named magnetometer stations." (2) "(Arithmetic: 10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT.)" The shorter lead sentence carries the claim; the parenthetical carries the audit trail. Total word count is similar; cognitive load is lower.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own predictions registry (raw-text/03-predictions.txt) contains a directly contradictory pair of August 2026 predictions: (a) per-station INTERMAGNET ΔB predictions of -5 to -26 nT (W004 baseline), implying via κ a non-zero SG Δg response of 3–16 µGal across stations, AND (b) E-PRED-C, listed as one of "4 Model-Discriminating Tests": "SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal." The dome is simultaneously committed to an SG-null prediction (E-PRED-C, 0.0 µGal) and an SG-pulse prediction (PRED-TIER-2/3, 10–13 µGal). These are incompatible. EXP-384 frames the implied ΔB premise as the load-bearing claim; a stronger move is to leverage the dome's OWN internal contradiction: "E-PRED-C, registered alongside the ΔB predictions in the same predictions.html dated 2026-03-12, predicts SG gravimeters will read 0.0 µGal at the August 2026 eclipse. PRED-TIER-2, registered 2026-04-04, predicts 10–13 µGal at C1/C4 contacts. The dome's own predictions registry contains both. Either the model predicts the SG null (consistent with 1999/2009 nulls but inconsistent with PRED-TIER-2) or it predicts the SG pulse (inconsistent with E-PRED-C and 1999/2009 nulls). The κ-coupling vise is now triple-pronged: 1999 + 2009 + dome's own 2026 E-PRED-C all point the same direction; only PRED-TIER-2's 10–13 µGal claim deviates." This is a major argument the section is not yet making.
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 17–24: per-station ΔB predictions registered 2026-03-12
data/predictions.json: PRED-TIER-2 "10–13 µGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts" registered 2026-04-04
sections.json:part6.html SEC-6.14 (would absorb this argument cleanly in the "What κ predicts during past eclipses" or vise subsection)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's W004 baseline (-22.24 nT) is itself derived from "empirical" sources the dome has not yet disclosed in raw-text. If the W004 baseline is from a single eclipse (or a small handful), the dome is using a single-event estimate as a global baseline for August 2026 — exactly the same single-event-numerator problem that drives WIN-058b/WIN-012 reviews. The section could note: "The W004 baseline of -22.24 nT — whose provenance the dome's predictions.html does not document in detail — is itself ~1.5× the Kim & Chang 207-measurement ensemble upper edge. The dome's implicit baseline is more anomalous than the K&C distribution suggests it should be, before κ even enters the picture." This is an argument made stronger, not weaker, by acknowledging the dome HAS published a baseline.
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 23: "Baselines: BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) | W004 empirical (-22.24 nT)"
WIN-058b prior reviews (κ numerator derived from single Halloween 2003 G5 storm — same single-event problem pattern)

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch correctly identifies that the dome's 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target is arithmetically self-consistent with κ = 1.677 only if a ΔB premise above the Kim & Chang typical totality ensemble is committed (data/sections.json:part6.html SEC-6.14 vise paragraph back-calc). The arithmetic is independently verifiable (10×1.677=16.770, 13×1.677=21.801; node-script-verified). The acknowledgment of the kernel-of-truth counter-arithmetic (10–13 nT typical-ensemble ΔB through κ-inverse yields 5.96–7.75 µGal, below target) is intellectually honest and demonstrates the analyst engaged the ISS-1957 author's objection head-on rather than dismissing it. The structural vise (κ universal → 1999/2009 refute, or κ has scope conditions → PRED-TIER-3 falls) is preserved and the back-calc tightens its second horn by making the implied-premise step explicit.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch's strength is also where the major hole sits: it does the back-calc correctly but mis-states what the dome has and hasn't published. The dome's predictions.html (raw-text/03-predictions.txt) does publish per-station ΔB predictions under two baselines, and the W004 baseline (-22.24 nT) is precisely the kind of above-ensemble premise the back-calc identifies. Once the patch is reworded to credit the dome's published W004 baseline as exactly the anomalous premise the back-calc requires, the vise gets stronger — the dome has already made the commitment the patch was treating as hypothetical, and that commitment is itself derived from a single eclipse (the W004 "empirical" label) which inherits all the single-event-numerator problems of κ's definition (WIN-012, WIN-058b). The kernel doesn't save the dome; the kernel needs to be more precisely stated.

text_fingerprint.section_html_length_note: part6.html length not recomputed this run; back-calc paragraph confirmed present at offset ~109,768
text_fingerprint.verdict_for_section: structural-argument-vise (κ-coupling cluster) with quantitative back-calc layer

deeper_analysis:
EXP-384 lands cleanly mechanically — the two anchor strings find unique matches in data/sections.json and data/wins.json, the arithmetic is verifiable, and the Kim & Chang cross-reference to WIN-068 ("the same 207-measurement ensemble") is accurate. The c4 verification PASS on the prior-cycle citation patches (Kim & Chang DOI .024→.022, Bernard→Hinderer, Monte Carlo softening) holds; this c5 pass does not re-litigate those.

The major hole is empirical, not structural: the patch's "unpublished premise" claim is contradicted by the dome's own raw-text/03-predictions.txt, captured 2026-03-12 (before EXP-384 was written). The dome publishes BOTH a typical-ensemble-consistent BOU baseline (-10.9 nT, yielding station Δg of 3–7.6 µGal under κ — well below target) AND an above-ensemble W004 empirical baseline (-22.24 nT, yielding station Δg of 6.1–15.6 µGal — spanning the 10–13 µGal target). The W004 baseline IS the anomalous premise the back-calc requires. Re-framing the paragraph from "the premise is unpublished" to "the premise is published, anomalous, and load-bearing" strengthens rather than weakens the structural vise. This is a minor_edit recommendation — one paragraph needs reworking, but the back-calc and Kim & Chang comparison both stay in.

A stronger argument the section is NOT yet making: raw-text/03-predictions.txt also publishes E-PRED-C ("SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal") as one of the four "Model-Discriminating Tests" for August 2026. This is the dome's own SG-null prediction for the upcoming eclipse, directly contradicting PRED-TIER-2/3's 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target. The dome's predictions registry contains an internal contradiction between these two SG predictions. The κ-cluster vise would be triple-pronged if this internal contradiction were surfaced: 1999 + 2009 SG nulls + 2026 dome-self-predicted SG null (E-PRED-C) all align against PRED-TIER-2's 10–13 µGal claim. Worth a new ISS or analyst expansion (separate from this EXP-384 review).

The defender-arithmetic example using 10–13 nT (rather than the ensemble bounds 5–15 nT) is a moderate weakness: the strongest defender push uses the ensemble upper edge (15 nT → 8.94 µGal, only ~1 µGal short of the 10 µGal lower target). The patch's current example understates how close a high-ensemble-edge premise can get.

The "inversely" framing on "Δg × κ = ΔB" is mathematically loose — same coupling, rearranged. Easy fix.

Carry-over: the c3 review's 15 open holes (ISS-1964/65/66/72/73/74/75/76/77, plus 5 new-in-c3 items) remain unaddressed by EXP-384. EXP-384 does NOT widen or worsen any of them; it operates in a paragraph that intersects the existing argument but does not collide with the open carry-forwards. Decider should track those separately.

Verdict: current_verdict_holds = true; the structural vise is intact and the back-calc strengthens it (subject to the major-hole reword). recommended_action = minor_edit: one paragraph requires precision repair; the back-calc and Kim & Chang ensemble comparison both remain. No verdict change.

summary_for_decider: Queue_id 366 (SEC-6.14 EXP-384 back-calc integration): current_verdict_holds=true, recommended_action=minor_edit, 1 major + 2 moderate + 2 minor holes + 2 stronger-argument notes. PASS on arithmetic and Kim & Chang cross-reference. FAIL on one empirical claim: the patch text says "that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located," but raw-text/03-predictions.txt (captured 2026-03-12 — BEFORE EXP-384 was drafted) publishes per-station August 2026 ΔB predictions under two baselines (BOU 2017 -10.9 nT; W004 empirical -22.24 nT), with W004 station values (Ebro -21.7, San Pablo -20.0, Coimbra -20.6, Chambon -20.6, Hartland -26.2) falling squarely in or above the back-calc's 16.8–21.8 nT band. The dome's W004 baseline IS the anomalous premise the patch identifies — published, not hypothetical. Reword the paragraph to credit the published W004 baseline as the load-bearing claim; the vise gets stronger, not weaker. Stronger-argument note: the dome's OWN E-PRED-C ("SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal") in the same predictions.html directly contradicts PRED-TIER-2/3's 10–13 µGal target — the κ-cluster section can leverage this internal contradiction (worth a separate analyst expansion / ISS). Defender-example sub-range (10–13 nT) is moderate weakness vs ensemble upper bound (15 nT → 8.94 µGal); "inversely" framing on Δg×κ=ΔB is moderately loose. All findings are precision repairs to the EXP-384 paragraph — no verdict-change, no structural rewrite needed. Recommend: open one ISS for the major rewording, track moderate/minor as a single grouped patch, and queue a separate analyst EXP for the E-PRED-C contradiction (deep-attack class). ISS-1957 itself can be closed: the back-calc is in, the kernel-of-truth was acknowledged, the defender pivot is addressed — just precision-tighten the wording.
